channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Overgrown brambles, nettles, untidy, 22nd June
item.description:
Overgrown brambles, nettles, even giant hogweed now which is dangerous to humans. Pathways blocked, more paths becoming impassable with overgrown brambles which pose a threat to dogs and wildlife. It'll only get worse and cost more to deal with. This park is looking in a very sorry state.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brook Way, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Park waste and fly tipping, 22nd June
item.description:
As above

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Grazebrook Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Big pothole, 22nd June
item.description:
Big pothole

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 48, Derby Hill, Forest Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Big black bags, 22nd June
item.description:
Big black bags dumped few days ago could you please collect it
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Church Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dropped Kurb, 22nd June
item.description:
Vehicle blocking the entrance to a dropped curb

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hardinge Road, Edmonton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Foxes continue to raid this bin every night, 22nd June
item.description:
This litter bin always been a problem but made worse in summer as people who pass throw away their takeaway wrappers etc in there for foxes to sniff out and drag out across the pavement and poo all over - disgusting ! Left for residents to clear up !’ Maybe a sign saying take your food wrapping home would help or as the bin full of dog poo anyway ( another issue- NOT a dog waste bin)
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mead Way, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pedestrian crossing outside Waitrose, Finchley Road, 22nd June
item.description:
There is a raised lump of bitumen slap band in the middle of the pedestrian crossing. My mother tripped on this on 22nd April and broke her shoulder, sustaining a three part proximal humerus fracture and she had to be admitted to 24/7 care.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Finchley Road, South Hampstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipped freezer., 22nd June
item.description:
Please remove a large fly tipped fridge freezer from outside Trinity Court, Sydenham Park, near the corner of Sydenham Park Road.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sydenham Park, Forest Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Could this large square pothole be inspected please?, 22nd June
item.description:
This pothole should be fixed if possible please
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Elmstead Lane, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Please inspect the pavement from 61 - 71 ElmsteadAve, 22nd June
item.description:
This section of paving should be inspected for cracked and uneven slabs
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Elmstead Avenue, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Can pavement be inspected at 51 Elmstead Ave, 22nd June
item.description:
Can the pavement here be inspected please?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Elmstead Avenue, Elmstead, Greater London

item.title: Dead tree at 26 Wanstead Rd, 22nd June
item.description:
Could this be investigated please?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Wanstead Road, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bins are eyesores and obstructing those with mobility needs & pushchairs, 22nd June
item.description:
This bins permanently on the pavement for long time and obstructing those with mobility needs. There are visually impaired people using the pavement all the time. This blind people are residents of Bradbury Oak House, Underhill Road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lordship Lane, Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: There are 6 to 7 grey sacks of rubbish on Phineas Pett Roadat the botof the alleyway (Kakehill) that leads to Ross Way. Similar sacks were left outside No 41 Ross Way earlier in the week, 21st June
item.description:
Grey sacks dumped on Phineas Pett Road between 33 and 34
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Phineas Pett Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken Picknic table, 21st June
item.description:
Broken picknic table and bench next to the Mardyke House. Someone also place the table on fire. Currently safery hazard. Need to be taking down or alternatively replaced.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crosslet Street, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flags attached to lamposts, 21st June
item.description:
A home in our local area is using their private flagpole to attaché bunting to a neighbouring public lampost.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Edward Tyler Road, Chinbrook, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped clothes, 21st June
item.description:
Pile of dumped clothes which have been there for at least two weeks.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Dickens Close, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grass is too long, 21st June
item.description:
Several residents reporting long grass all around the area. This are is long and needs to be maintained to keep the area nice. Residents with dogs fear for vet bills due to the head seeds, and children can't play on the green. It's a joke, poor roads and poor maintenance. What are we paying council tax for?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 82, Doveney Close, St Paul's Cray, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pedestrian guardrail was damaged, 21st June
item.description:
The pedestrian guardrail in Lordship Lane & Wood Vale was damaged for almost 2weeks. It needs an immediate repair for health & safety of the pedestrians.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lordship Lane, Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bricks in kerb, 21st June
item.description:
Resident has put bricks in gutter to access the, property 116 Carlton road erith kent needs to apply for dropped kerb £££
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Carlton Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
